one_liner: Salesforce provides a broad, mature REST/SOAP/GraphQL API surface with self-serve free Developer Edition access and OAuth2 authentication, plus emerging (pilot/beta) official MCP servers for AI agent integration.

auth_methods.notes: Recommended method via External Client Apps or Connected Apps; access token used in Authorization header for REST/GraphQL APIs.
SOAP API login() call uses username/password plus security token to obtain a session ID; disabled by default on new orgs.

self_serve_notes: Free Developer Edition orgs are available via self-serve signup at developer.salesforce.com with no contract required, providing full API access (REST, SOAP, GraphQL, Bulk, Tooling, Metadata) and Agentforce/MCP capabilities, subject to standard API call limits.

api_surface.notes: Salesforce exposes REST, SOAP, GraphQL, Bulk, Tooling, and Metadata APIs across its platform, covering SObjects, queries (SOQL/GraphQL), and org management.

main_blocker: Salesforce's official Hosted MCP Server is still in pilot, so production-grade MCP integration may require using the Beta DX MCP server or building a custom MCP wrapper around the stable REST/GraphQL/SOAP APIs in the interim.

evidence.supports: OAuth 2.0 via Connected/External Client Apps is the primary recommended auth method for Salesforce APIs.
SOAP API login() call supports username/password + security token authentication, disabled by default on new orgs.
GraphQL API accepts OAuth access token in authorization header, indicating broad modern API surface.
Free, self-serve Developer Edition signup with full platform and API access, including Agentforce and Data 360.
Salesforce Developer Edition now includes MCP support alongside Agentforce Vibes IDE and Claude 4.5 integration.
Developers can query REST API Limits resource to monitor API allocation, confirming self-serve API usage model.

flags: MCP hosted server still in pilot status, not GA
DX MCP server is in Beta and developer/ops-focused rather than general business-data focused
Multiple API paradigms (REST/SOAP/GraphQL/Bulk) increase integration complexity though all are self-serve
